← all tutorials

Backpressure & Flow Control — interactive 📖 BACKPRESSURE.md 📄 backpressure.py

📖 Full guide (.md, green) — the long-form written explanation.  📄 Source code (.py, orange) — the ground-truth logic this page is verified against.
A producer (P req/s) feeds a queue, which a consumer (C req/s) drains. When P > C the backlog grows at (P − C) forever — until OOM and cascading timeouts. Backpressure stops that. Toggle unbounded (runs away → red) vs bounded (rejects at K → stays put) vs reactive (consumer pulls N). Defaults match backpressure.py Section A/B (P=100, C=50, K=1000).

① The pipeline — watch the queue fill, saturate, or run away

What to do: pick a mode, set P / C / K, press ▶ run. The queue bar grows at (P−C). Unbounded: it never stops (turns red, "→ ∞"). Bounded: it hits the gold wall K and the surplus is rejected (gray) — depth stays put, goodput = C. Reactive: the consumer pulls N items at a time; in-flight never exceeds N.
paused · t = 0 s
queued backlog near the wall overflow (unbounded) cap K (the wall) rejected
In words: goodput (work actually done) is always C — the consumer is the bottleneck either way. Backpressure changes nothing about goodput; it caps memory and fails fast. Formula: depth(t) = (P−C)·t (unbounded), min(K, (P−C)·t) (bounded).

② Token bucket — burst cap R, sustained rate r

What this shows: a bucket of R tokens refilling at r/s. Each request costs 1 token; empty → reject. A burst drains up to R instantly (the burst cap); a sustained stream at P>r admits exactly r/s. Press ▶ hit burst to drain the bucket.
tokens = 20 / 20 (full)
Sustained: throughput = min(P, r); reject rate = (P − r)/P. Token bucket lets short bursts through (up to R); leaky bucket (the strict cousin) does not.

③ Priority load shedding — drop LOW, protect HIGH

What this shows: the service budget = C req/s. Serve HIGH first, then MEDIUM, then LOW; shed the rest. The loss is graduated — payments lose nothing, analytics take the hit. Without priority every class loses the same fraction.
Same total goodput (= C), but the blast radius is tiny: the critical path stays green while the cheap work degrades. This is the bulkhead + graceful-degradation pattern (Hystrix / Resilience4j).

④ Gold check — bounded vs unbounded over 1 hour, recomputed live in JS

What this proves: the page re-runs the closed-form fluid model in your browser — depth=(P−C)·t vs min(K,(P−C)·t) over a full hour — on the identical parameters (P=100, C=50, K=1000) as backpressure.py GOLD CHECK. The green check: OK below confirms the queue stays bounded with backpressure while goodput is identical.
check: … JS fluid recompute matches backpressure.py GOLD CHECK (unbounded→180000, bounded→1000, goodput both=180000, ratio=180×).
